name: Hall Effect Sensor
fullName: A3144 Hall Effect Magnetic Sensor Module
tagline: Detects magnetic fields and outputs a digital signal — used for RPM counting and magnetic proximity sensing
description: A Hall Effect sensor detects the presence of a magnetic field and outputs a digital HIGH or LOW signal. The A3144 is a popular unipolar Hall sensor that outputs LOW when a south pole magnet is brought close, and HIGH when the magnet is removed. It's used for counting rotations (RPM), detecting motor position, detecting open/close states of doors, and triggering on magnetic targets.

specs.outputType: Open-collector digital output (active LOW with magnet)
specs.packageType: TO-92 (3-pin) or breakout module with pull-up resistor

pinout.name: Output
pinout.desc: Digital output — LOW when magnet is detected, HIGH otherwise (needs pull-up resistor on bare sensor)

wiringNote: The bare A3144 TO-92 sensor requires a 10kΩ pull-up resistor between the output pin and VCC. Most breakout modules include this resistor. Use INPUT_PULLUP if using the bare sensor with Arduino internal pull-up.
commonMistakes: Forgetting pull-up resistor on bare sensor → output floats, false triggers
Using wrong magnet pole — A3144 responds to south pole only (north pole = no response on unipolar sensors)
Mounting magnet too far away → sensor won't trigger (typical range: <5mm)
Confusing Hall sensor with IR sensor — Hall responds to magnetic fields, not light
